text: Gruha Lakshmi Scheme (Karnataka): Monthly financial assistance of ₹2,000 to the woman head of a family. Eligibility: Women heads of households with BPL/APL/Antyodaya cards. Benefits: Direct benefit transfer to bank accounts to empower women and support household expenses.

Shakti Scheme (Karnataka): Free travel for women in non-premium government buses within Karnataka. Eligibility: All women and transgender persons who are residents of Karnataka. Benefits: Eliminates travel costs for commuting to work, education, or personal needs.

Anna Bhagya (Karnataka): Provides 10kg of free food grains to every member of a BPL household. Eligibility: BPL and Antyodaya card holders. Benefits: Ensures food security for vulnerable families through the Public Distribution System.

Yuva Nidhi (Karnataka): Unemployment allowance for graduates and diploma holders. Eligibility: Unemployed youth who graduated in the last two years. Benefits: ₹3,000 per month for graduates and ₹1,500 per month for diploma holders for up to 24 months.

Gruha Jyothi (Karnataka): Free electricity up to 200 units per month for domestic households. Eligibility: All domestic consumers in Karnataka. Benefits: Significant reduction in monthly utility bills for middle and low-income families.

Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thogai (Tamil Nadu): Monthly honorarium of ₹1,000 for women heads of families. Eligibility: Women from eligible households meeting income and landholding criteria. Benefits: Financial independence and social security for women.

Pudhumai Penn Scheme (Tamil Nadu): Monthly financial assistance for girl students for higher education. Eligibility: Girls who studied in Government schools from Class 6 to 12. Benefits: ₹1,000 per month until completion of degree or diploma to prevent dropouts.

Naan Mudhalvan (Tamil Nadu): Skill development and career guidance for youth. Eligibility: Students in schools and colleges across Tamil Nadu. Benefits: Access to industry-relevant courses, training, and placement opportunities in emerging sectors.
Majhi Ladki Bahin Yojana (Maharashtra): Financial support for women. Eligibility: Women aged 21-65 with family income < 2.5 lakh. Benefits: Monthly allowance of ₹1,500 directly into bank accounts.

Mukhyamantri Annapurna Scheme (Maharashtra): Providing free LPG cylinders. Eligibility: Beneficiaries of Ladki Bahin and Ujjwala schemes. Benefits: Three free LPG cylinders per year to eligible households.

Mukhyamantri Yuva Karya Prashikshan Yojana (Maharashtra): Internship and stipend for youth. Eligibility: Unemployed youth with 12th pass, diploma, or degree. Benefits: Stipend ranging from ₹6,000 to ₹10,000 per month for on-the-job training.
PM-DevINE: Development Initiative for North Eastern Region. Benefits: Funding for infrastructure and social development projects in the NE states.
SANKALP: Skill Acquisition and Knowledge Awareness for Livelihood Promotion. Benefits: Strengthening institutional mechanisms for skill development and increasing access to quality training.
STRIVE: Skills Strengthening for Industrial Value Enhancement. Benefits: Improving the relevance and efficiency of skills training provided through ITIs and apprenticeships.
National Beekeeping Mission: Promotion of scientific beekeeping for income and crop productivity. Benefits: Subsidies for bee colonies, hives, and honey extraction units.
Mission Indradhanush: Universal immunization program. Benefits: Vaccines against 12 vaccine-preventable diseases for children and pregnant women.

Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation (AMRUT): Infrastructure for water supply and sewerage in urban areas. Benefits: Improved water supply, sewerage networks, and development of green spaces in cities.
Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Kendra (PMKK): Model training centers for skill development. Benefits: State-of-the-art infrastructure for industry-driven vocational training.
National Rural Livelihoods Mission (NRLM): Promoting self-employment and wage employment. Benefits: Financial support and training for Self Help Groups (SHGs) in rural areas.
Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-NULM: Urban poverty alleviation. Benefits: Skill training, credit support for self-employment, and support for street vendors in urban areas.
Pradhan Mantri Mahila Shakti Kendra (PMMSK): Empowering rural women through community participation. Benefits: One-stop centers providing information on health, education, and social security.
National Social Assistance Programme (NSAP): Social security for the elderly, widows, and persons with disabilities. Benefits: Monthly pensions under IGNOAPS, IGNWPS, and IGNDPS.

Pradhan Mantri Khanij Kshetra Kalyan Yojana (PMKKKY): Welfare of people in mining-affected areas. Benefits: Infrastructure development and social welfare projects in areas impacted by mining operations.
National Career Service (NCS): One-stop portal for employment services. Benefits: Job matching, career counseling, and information on training and internships for job seekers.
Pradhan Mantri Gram Sadak Yojana (PMGSY): Rural road connectivity. Benefits: Construction of all-weather roads to connect unconnected habitations in rural India.
Kisan Credit Card (KCC) Scheme: Providing timely credit to farmers. Benefits: Short-term credit for cultivation, post-harvest expenses, and investment credit for agriculture.
Rashtriya Swasthya Bima Yojana (RSBY): Health insurance for unorganized sector workers. Benefits: Smart card-based health insurance cover up to ₹30,000 for BPL families.
National Bamboo Mission: Holistic development of the bamboo sector. Benefits: Support for bamboo cultivation, processing, and marketing to increase farmer income.
National Mission for Sustainable Agriculture (NMSA): Climate-resilient agriculture. Benefits: Promotion of rainfed area development, soil health management, and climate-smart practices.
Pradhan Mantri Van Dhan Yojana: Livelihood generation for tribals. Benefits: Formation of Van Dhan Vikas Kendras for value addition of Minor Forest Produce (MFP).
National SC-ST Hub: Supporting SC-ST entrepreneurs. Benefits: Capacity building, market linkage, and financial support for SC-ST owned enterprises.
Scheme for Higher Education Youth in Apprenticeship and Skills (SHREYAS): Industry apprenticeship for graduates. Benefits: Monthly stipend and training for students in non-technical courses.
Global Initiative of Academic Networks (GIAN): Tapping international faculty for higher education. Benefits: Short-term courses by international faculty in Indian universities to improve quality.
SWAYAM (Study Webs of Active-Learning for Young Aspiring Minds): Massive Open Online Courses (MOOCs). Benefits: Free high-quality online courses with certification from top institutions.
Impactful Policy Research in Social Science (IMPRESS): Funding for social science research. Benefits: Support for high-quality research projects to inform policy decisions.
Innovation in Science Pursuit for Inspired Research (INSPIRE): Attracting talent to science. Benefits: Scholarships and fellowships for students pursuing science at various levels.
Vigyan Jyoti Scheme: Encouraging girls to pursue STEM careers. Benefits: Counseling, workshops, and mentoring for girl students in Science and Technology.
Kiran Scheme (Knowledge Involvement in Research Advancement through Nurturing): Supporting women scientists. Benefits: Fellowships, mobility support, and infrastructure grants for women in science.
National Supercomputing Mission: Developing a grid of supercomputers in India. Benefits: High-performance computing infrastructure for scientific and societal applications.
SANKALP Scheme: Strengthening Skill Acquisition and Knowledge Awareness. Benefits: Improving the quality and market-relevance of vocational training across India.
NIDHI (National Initiative for Developing and Harnessing Innovations): Supporting startups and innovators. Benefits: Funding for proof-of-concept, incubation, and scaling of innovations.
Skill India Mission: Training over 40 crore people in different skills by 2022. Benefits: Access to various skill development schemes and placement opportunities.
PM-CARES for Children: Support for children orphaned by COVID-19. Benefits: Monthly stipend, health insurance, and a corpus of ₹10 lakh upon reaching age 23.

Mission Vatsalya: A roadmap for child protection and child welfare services. Benefits: Support for children in difficult circumstances, institutional care, and non-institutional care through sponsorship and foster care.
